key_strengths: Clearly understandable business model with identifiable revenue drivers (GMV, take rate, credit quality, funding costs)
Proprietary real-time underwriting models producing 2.7% delinquency rates vs 8.8% for credit cards
Affirm Card growing explosively (3.7M users, 159% GMV growth) creating consumer switching costs
Amazon partnership locked through 2031 provides merchant-side stability
Gross margins expanding rapidly from 45% (FY2023) to 67.5% (FY2025) with emerging operating leverage
key_risks: Credit cycle vulnerability — business never tested through a consumer credit downturn, rising provisions (up 40% YoY)
No margin of safety at $47.69 — stock trades 64% above base-case IV ($29) with inverted risk-reward
Capital market dependency — $8.8B loan book requires continuous warehouse facility and securitization access
Competitive commoditization — Walmart defection to Klarna proves merchants treat BNPL as switchable input
SBC-masked profitability — $321M SBC dwarfs $52M net income in FY2025
red_flags: Interest coverage of 1.1x is dangerously thin
ROIC of 2.8% is well below cost of capital
SBC is 6.1x reported net income — equity dilution is the real cost of profitability
Provision for credit losses accelerating 40% YoY
Beta of 3.73 with 95% historical peak-to-trough drawdown
Dual-class structure gives CEO 44.4% voting control with no practical shareholder recourse
buy_triggers: Stock price below $20 providing genuine margin of safety near conservative IV
Sustained $500M+ annual net income for 2+ consecutive years proving durability
Affirm Card reaching 10M+ active users demonstrating consumer finance platform shift
sell_triggers: 30+ day delinquency rates exceeding 4% for two consecutive quarters
Loss of a top-3 merchant relationship (especially Amazon)
Regulatory action structurally impairing the interest-bearing BNPL model
valuation_summary: At $47.69 and $15.9B market cap, AFRM trades at 4.3x sales, 34.7x FCF, and 44.6x EV/EBITDA on trailing numbers. Bear-case IV is $8/share, base-case $29, bull-case $73. The market prices in $600-800M sustainable net income within 3-5 years — a 20x jump from FY2025's $52M — requiring near-perfect execution with no credit cycle disruption.
compact_checklist: Affirm lends money to shoppers at checkout, earning merchant fees and consumer interest on installment loans, with 96% of transactions coming from repeat users who split purchases across 478K active merchants including Amazon (locked through 2031).

Proprietary per-transaction underwriting models (producing 2.7% delinquency rates vs 8.8% for credit cards) and the fast-growing Affirm Card (3.7M users, 159% GMV growth) are real advantages, but merchants can and do switch providers — as Walmart's defection to Klarna proved.

ROIC is a meager 2.8% after years of losses, gross margins are expanding (67.5%), but SBC of $321M dwarfs net income of $52M, and the $8.8B loan book requires continuous capital market access to fund — this is a leveraged lender, not an asset-light platform.

Levchin is a credible founder-operator with modest $538K cash compensation and SBC is declining as a share of revenue (28% to 10%), but his 44.4% voting control through dual-class shares and $113M in recent stock sales with zero purchases weaken the alignment story.

Interest coverage of 1.1x and $7.9B in securitized debt that requires constant warehouse facility renewals mean a credit market freeze or consumer downturn would simultaneously hit revenue, increase losses, and restrict funding — a survivable but painful triple threat.

At $47.69 (base-case IV of $29, bear-case IV of $8), the market must believe Affirm will reach $600-800M in sustainable net income within 3-5 years — requiring 20x the current earnings with no credit cycle disruption.
There is none — the stock trades 64% above base-case IV with inverted risk-reward (83% downside to bear case vs 53% upside to bull case), and a rational entry point is below $20.
30+ day delinquency rates exceeding 4% for two consecutive quarters, or the loss of the Amazon partnership, would confirm the credit risk and merchant fragility that the current price ignores.
source_summary: Affirm Q2 FY2026 earnings (Feb 2026), FY2025 annual report, 10-Q filings, yfinance auto-fetched financials, Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat, Alpha Spread, Investing.com, Simply Wall St, TipRanks, Payments Dive, CNBC, TradingView. Data current as of March 2026.
